raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"deepening the yawning chasm opening up between Washington and its closest allies over the Arctic territory.","explanation":"Uses a dramatic metaphor (“yawning chasm”) to amplify conflict, injecting interpretive, sensational framing in the author’s voice without evidence in the piece."},{"biasType":"Subjective Qualifying Adjectives","biasedText":"during a rambling speech to world leaders at the World Economic Forum in the Swiss town of Davos on Wednesday.","explanation":"“Rambling” is a subjective, evaluative descriptor of Trump’s speech quality presented by the author without attribution."},{"biasType":"Unsubstantiated claims","biasedText":"Alliance officials have repeatedly said a U.S. military attack on Greenland, a semi-autonomous part of Denmark, would spell the end of NATO.","explanation":"Vague sourcing (“Alliance officials”) and a sweeping prediction (“spell the end of NATO”) are presented without specific names, quotes, or citations."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"The prospect of the U.S. — the most influential and powerful member of NATO —attacking territory belonging to a fellow alliance member has always been unfathomable, until now.","explanation":"Asserts an absolute historical judgment (“always been unfathomable”) and a turning point (“until now”) as fact without evidence or attribution."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"which would be a violation of international law.","explanation":"Presents a categorical legal conclusion in the author’s voice without sourcing or legal analysis."}]}

detections.biasedText: deepening the yawning chasm opening up between Washington and its closest allies over the Arctic territory.
detections.explanation: Uses a dramatic metaphor (“yawning chasm”) to amplify conflict, injecting interpretive, sensational framing in the author’s voice without evidence in the piece.

detections.biasedText: during a rambling speech to world leaders at the World Economic Forum in the Swiss town of Davos on Wednesday.
detections.explanation: “Rambling” is a subjective, evaluative descriptor of Trump’s speech quality presented by the author without attribution.

detections.biasedText: Alliance officials have repeatedly said a U.S. military attack on Greenland, a semi-autonomous part of Denmark, would spell the end of NATO.
detections.explanation: Vague sourcing (“Alliance officials”) and a sweeping prediction (“spell the end of NATO”) are presented without specific names, quotes, or citations.

detections.biasedText: The prospect of the U.S. — the most influential and powerful member of NATO —attacking territory belonging to a fellow alliance member has always been unfathomable, until now.
detections.explanation: Asserts an absolute historical judgment (“always been unfathomable”) and a turning point (“until now”) as fact without evidence or attribution.

detections.biasedText: which would be a violation of international law.
detections.explanation: Presents a categorical legal conclusion in the author’s voice without sourcing or legal analysis.
